candidates.stmt:
A little bit about me: I live in Hillingdon, West London, and have proudly served as an NHS GP in a practice in the neighbouring constituency of Hayes and Harlington for more than 20 years. My commitment to public service extends beyond healthcare, as I also serve as a School Governor for a local school in Hayes and as a Charity Trustee for a local organisation, that supports older people and those in need across Hillingdon.

Born in Barnet, North London, I attended a primary school in Colindale before my family returned to India. It was there that I spent my childhood and youth and pursued my dreams of becoming a doctor. I have been a resident of Hillingdon since 2016,  living with my husband, in-laws, and my children.

So why am I asking for your support? 

Throughout my entire career, I have been deeply involved in public service. As a GP, I have not only listened to my patients but also acted as their advocate. I have worked in various hospitals around the country including West Middlesex hospital where I worked as a junior doctor in the maternity unit. I have been instrumental in policy formulation and service redesign as a Clinical Leader in North West London, and worked closely with GP colleagues in Hounslow. Additionally, my role as a Board Member of a large Mental Health and Community NHS Trust, as well as my position as a Lead for Nursing Homes in my practice area, have provided me with diverse perspectives on healthcare and social care issues.

My experience as a School Governor has given me valuable insight into our education system and the challenges it faces. These experiences, together with my ability to empathise, listen, and understand people's needs, have led me to believe that politics is the arena where I can make a tangible difference, which is why I started my journey over a year ago. I am eager to utilise my personal and professional skills, accumulated knowledge, and understanding of people's concerns to serve you and the wider society.

I in

My name is James Bourke, I am one of the three Conservative candidates standing for election as a borough councillor in South Lake Ward, Woodley.

I've been a student at the University of Reading for a few years now, and for much longer than this I have have known the area through my uncle living here and frequently visiting. In all this time I've become very familiar with Woodley, with all it's great amenities and pleasant neighbourhoods.

Sadly in the last two years this idyllic town has become blighted by poor decisions from Wokingham Borough Council. The roads have become yet poorer in quality, parking charges have risen (they plan to charge you to park on the roadside), council services have been slashed or eliminated, anti-social behaviour has become worse around the Woodley Precinct, and now they want to reduce your weekly bin collections to a bi-weekly schedule.

As a borough councillor, my goal and that of my colleagues will be to reverse or stop the above issues as a matter of urgency and restore respect in the council for the people of Woodley and South Lake. A vote for us, for me, is a vote to get our town back on track.

I grew up here, and genuinely care about our Community.   As your Councillor I will work tirelessly to promote local issues & stand up for what matters to us.  I will fight for safer & cleaner streets, to challenge further inappropriate HMOs, & to prioritise local housing for local people. It’s time for change.

wards.results_url: https://pre.hillingdon.gov.uk/elections-voting/hillingdon-council-elections-2026/5 (spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
I’m proud to stand as your Reform candidate for Harefield Village in the upcoming local elections.

I am 55 years old, a married father of three, and a professional surveyor with years of hands-on experience in planning and development.

I’ve lived in Harefield for the past 20 years. I’ve been active in our village for several years especially at the Harefield Tenants and Residents Association (HTRA). For the past two years, I have served as the Chairman for the HTRA working directly with residents to protect and improve our community. I am also an officer in the Harefield Royal British Legion, supporting those who have served our country.

I’m not a career politician — I’m someone who has been actively standing up for Harefield for years.

My priorities are clear:

- Stop the overdevelopment of HMOs. I will oppose inappropriate new HMOs and push for stronger controls to protect the character of our village. Existing HMOs must be properly managed and held to account.

- Fix our roads and footpaths. Residents deserve safe, well-maintained infrastructure. I will hold the council accountable for long-overdue repairs.

- Tackle fly-tipping and anti-social behaviour. Stronger enforcement and real consequences are needed to protect our environment.

- Stand up for Harefield’s future. I strongly oppose the proposed large-scale mosque development at Widewater Place and will continue to represent the concerns of local residents.

I have been front and centre in these issues, working on your behalf, not just during election time but every day.
I will endeavour to integrate and bring together constituants with results and aim to serve the Community.

Wayne was born and raised in Northolt and has lived and worked in the local area all his life.  He is married with a ten-year-old daughter and has worked in the Freight Forwarding Industry for the past 15 years.

Wayne has been a Hillingdon Councillor since 2010.  He has a proven record of securing substantial investment for residents and transforming their lives: From initiatives for Older Residents to the installation of Outdoor Gyms and Children’s playground refurbishment, and championing Green Spaces for all.  Wayne has extensive experience of serving the residents of H&H.  He has Chaired the Social Services, Housing and Public Health Policy Overview Committee and is the Chairman of the Residents, Education & Environmental Services Policy Overview Committee, ensuring the rights of local residents are protected and enhanced.

Wayne has always been a Brexiter.  In the 2016 European Referendum he was involved in the Local Leave campaign and was delighted when 58.25% of Hayes and Harlington voted Leave.  He has become increasingly frustrated by the local MP who has consistently delayed this process and ignored the majority of constituents who voted to leave the EU.

Wayne is completely committed to ensuring Britain’s withdrawal from the European Union and seizing the opportunity to unleash Britain’s economic potential in the wider world and creating local prosperity for people in Hayes & Harlington.

A NEW LOCAL COMMUNITY CHAMPION FOR HILLINGDON EAST WARD: 

https://www.hillingdonconservatives.org/news/kelly-martin-contest-hillingdon-east-election

- Kelly has lived locally in Hillingdon for over a decade  - Trustee of Fassnidge Trust: Committed to supporting the elderly community in Hillingdon  - Volunteers at Harefield Hospital  - A lead resident on engagement with Housing Management Company - Has lead initiatives on enhancing living standards and community cohesion. 

Kelly will form part of a strong team of Conservative Councillors in Hillingdon East if elected.

SEE MY CAMPAIGN LITERATURE AT: https://www.hillingdonconservatives.org/news/kelly-martin-contest-hillingdon-east-election

For too long the electorate has been taken for granted and ignored. Politicians work for the people, not the other way around. This country needs voices in local government and Parliament that will challenge the big parties and speak for the people who voted them in. Enough is enough.

I've lived in Hillingdon for over 25 years; nearly all of my family live here. I never wanted to get involved in Politics but I became disillusioned with the current political system and wanted to do something to change it for the better.

I served in the British Army, being deployed to the Middle East during the first Gulf War. I'm now fighting again, but this time for the sake of those that hold Britain and its values close to their hearts. Together, we can fix this. I believe in Family, Industry & Nation.

Being a lifelong Hillingdon resident, I have seen the excellent work the Conservative Council have done for many years, and I wish to build on that.

I am proud to see how tidy and well kept my local area is, and I know that is due to the hard work of Conservative Councillors who ensure our bins are collected weekly, and our services are well run. I equally see the difference of Labour run Ealing and Hounslow Council who charge their residents a lot more money, and for a poorer quality of services.

I hope I can rely on your support to ensure that we retain Conservative Councillors and that our much loved services are protected and maintained to the standard that we deserve.

I’m standing as your Liberal Democrat candidate for Pinkwell because I care deeply about our community and the issues that affect everyday life here in Hayes.

Like many of you, I want to see a cleaner, safer and fairer neighbourhood—where families feel secure, young people have opportunities, and everyone is listened to.

If elected, I will focus on:

* Tackling fly-tipping and improving street cleanliness
* Working with police to reduce anti-social behaviour
* Pushing for better housing standards and accountability
* Supporting local schools and opportunities for young people
* Making sure residents’ voices are heard at the council

I believe local politics should be about people. I will always listen, respond, and stand up for what matters to you.

Together, we can make Pinkwell an even better place to live.

If you see me around, please stop and have a chat—I’d love to hear your concerns and ideas.

A councillor in Ruislip since 1993, Philip is married with four children.  Philip is an active campaigner against inappropriate development in Ruislip, to preserve the attractive appearance of our town. He works with the local safer neighbourhood team to tackle crime and anti-social behaviour. He is also Chairman of the school Governors at the Sacred Heart School in Ruislip.

Reform represents local people, but not as career politicians, as local citizens and business people who suffer the same crime, poor services, limited facilities and rising local taxes you do. 
We know what it means to put the customer (you) first. 
We know your needs come before the needs of the council. 
We know a near £600m debt and £422k per month interest payment is not the way to rescue a council - it's the road to bankruptcy. Reform would never have let this happen. We will run the borough efficiently and for the benefit of you, the customers. 
We will return to safety, run efficiently, remove waste, improve integration, address local concerns around HMOs, education, crime, anti-social behaviour. 
The customer comes first - not politics, not ideology, you! 
We will work with you and for you, because we are you.

Born in Eastcote, I went to Southbourne Comprehensive School – which I left with an “O” level in English. For most of my early years I worked 2 jobs - Toilet Cleaner, Floor Cleaner, Butcher then Baggage Handler at Heathrow. I worked very hard, had a bit of luck & have now done reasonably well in life. I’m married & have a son.

In the early 80’s I worked as a Baggage Handler at Heathrow Airport. I was a member of the T&GW’s Union. I valued the protections a trade union gave me! I was equally inspired by the “then” Conservatives vision & personal belief they generated within me. I joined the local YC’s group, eventually became their Chairman (not stereo typical!) and built it into the largest group in the country.

I’ve a genuine focus on our frontline caring professionals & Police. They must be properly paid & supported. Without them our society will continue to crumble! They should be a priority!

Our young working-class people must be well educated, skilled & ADVISED, schooled in financial management, building their self-belief & guiding them to achieve well thought through ACHIEABLE visions. Things I wished I’d not had to figure out myself! Belief is All!

Our elderly. They have paid into the system all their lives & generally experienced full employment. They must be supported through easy access to the public services they require – they have paid for them! They must feel safe in their own homes. They must feel their local area is being maintained & resembles a society they enjoy & still relate to.

I struggle to understand what has been allowed to happen to this country. What have our politicians been doing!!

I can’t associate with either of the two main parties anymore. I don’t believe either of them. Since 1997 Labour bankrupted the country & then, the so-called Conservatives have continued to wreck it!

I find their promises patronising, desperate & insincere. I feel they are both taking us for fools. Many have achieved very little on a personal basis – ha

Svavar is proud to call Ruislip Gardens and South Ruislip home. Having lived in the area since 2001, Svavar felt a sense of belonging from the moment he arrived. Surrounded by natural beauty and welcoming neighbours, Svavar values the green spaces and the kindness he encounters throughout the community.

In his professional life, Svavar works in IT for a large multinational corporation. He is experienced in building consensus, bringing people together around shared goals, and leading with empathy. Svavar enjoys hearing a range of perspectives and is unafraid to be persistent when it matters.

Svavar considers himself fortunate to live comfortably, and he wants the same security and quality of life for everyone in the community. He believes that life improves for all when people support one another, and that local government has a vital role to play in making everyday life better. In particular, Svavar is committed to ensuring that social and elder care is delivered with compassion, that vulnerable residents receive the support they need, and that children have the best possible start in life. Alongside this, Svavar champions excellent outdoor spaces, strong local businesses, and a thriving community. He believes there is real hope for positive change, and that working together is how it happens.

I am a dedicated local campaigner focused on getting the basics right and delivering practical results for residents.

Regularly out and about in the community, listening to residents, taking up local concerns, I push for action on the issues that matter most, like fly tipping, anti-social behaviour and opening Uxbridge police station.

If I am lucky enough to become your councillor, I will work hard for all residents, reply to all correspondence from residents quickly and use my knowledge of running a successful business to scrutinise the council to make sure Uxbridge gets the best return on investment.

Vote for me, Terence Murray, for a Councillor who will work hard, listen to residents, and get things done.

A tireless advocate for Uxbridge with a focus on getting the basics right and getting things done for residents.

The driving force behind campaigns to Save Mount Vernon’s Urgent Care Unit and force a rethink on the closure of Hayes Police Station last year.

Regularly out and about with residents in Uxbridge and taking action on concerns - with hundreds of pieces of fly tipping removed and leading on initiatives to restore pride in Uxbridge.

Vote for Riley Russell in Uxbridge Ward for a Councillor that gets things done.

Anil is local to the area and has worked as a cardiologist and acute physician for nearly 30 years, with a lot of that time at Hillingdon Hospital. He is now semi-retired and focusing on work in the community.

Vote for Anil for a considerate Conservative councillor who works hard for residents and puts them first.

Manifesto Launched
 
Next exciting news is that we now have our manifesto completed thanks to the work led by Tony Little and his team.  You can read our manifesto online Hillingdon Liberal Democrats. It is a comprehensive blueprint for a better Hillingdon which I hope you will enjoy reading.

Sajid Iqbal has legal background. He has qualified advocate and Bar member of Khayber Pakhtun Khwa Pakistan.

Manifesto:
* https://www.hillingdonlibdems.org.uk/council-elections-2026/our-manifesto-for-hillingdon

I Have been a campaigner in Hillingdon for many years, and now work as an MDO for the trade union GMB in Hayes focusing on areas including the gig economy and transport, including professional drivers. I have been a key figure rallying Uber drivers to fight for rights and recognition – all the way to the successful challenge at the Supreme Court.

I am a strong campaigner and has proven my ability to achieve change.

Some of my achievements locally have been two successful challenges to Telephone mast installations as well as  a successful petition to seek changes on the B466.

I chair and am a Chair & Trustee of a local charity sit on TUC LESE committees including Transport and International Affairs..

Residents need a strong advocate and I vow to be their champion and their voice.

I have a record as someone who stands up to injustice and if elected, I will do that diligently for residents.

Already as a candidate I am advocating for issues that are being presented to me by residents ably assisted by my Labour group team members. These include fly tipping and community safety as a ward policing panel member.

I live in Hayes and work in local
government, where I focus on
improving services and
supporting residents. I
understand how council
decisions affect daily life, and I
use my experience to speak up
for fair and practical action.
I am already involved in raising
concerns about safety, housing
conditions, cleaner streets and
better local services. I listen to
residents, follow up on issues
and work to get real solutions.
For Wood End Ward, I want to
help create safer
neighbourhoods, improve the
local environment, support
families and young people and
make sure the council uses
money wisely. My aim is to be a
strong voice for the community
and to put residents first.

As a Hayes resident of over 20 years and a Wood End local, I am standing as an Independent candidate to provide strong, practical representation for our community.

My professional background includes working within the council’s Parking, Highways, Community Safety and Anti-Social Behaviour teams, followed by my current role as a Community Housing Officer. This experience has given me first-hand knowledge of the issues residents face and how the system works.

Independent of party politics, I am committed to putting residents first and delivering real results for Hayes and Wood End.

Since 2004, I have lived in Hayes, a community I proudly call home. That same year, I began working at Heathrow Airport, where I served until 2013. At Heathrow, there is a saying: “No two days are the same at Heathrow Airport.” That phrase did more than describe the workplace it became a philosophy for my life. Every day brings change, challenge, and opportunity. I firmly believe that we must adapt and evolve with the times, or we risk living in the past.

In 2013, my life changed when I was diagnosed with Multiple Sclerosis. While this was a significant turning point, it strengthened my resilience and deepened my perspective on life, community, and service.
My reason for wanting to become a councillor is simple to give back. My journey through family values, hard work, personal challenge, and lived experience, This has prepared me to serve my community with understanding, adaptability, and commitment. I believe in progress, in community strength, and in ensuring that the knowledge we gain in life is used to uplift others.
I believe in telling the truth,  Admitting when you get something wrong, Then to find a solution together to overcome the issue.

Elizabeth (Ella) Garelick is a current councillor dedicated public servant, business owner, and trade unionist with over a three decades of experience in corporate management and community advocacy.

Currently serving as a Local Councillor for the London Borough of Hillingdon, representing the Wood End ward. Proven track record in advocating for residents' rights, affordable housing, and high-quality community services. Combines strong commercial acumen as a Company Director with a deep commitment to social justice through roles in the GMB Union and previously with Hillingdon Law Centre.

My Core Competencies include:
•Public Policy & Governance: Experienced in local government decision-making, planning regulations, and resident services. I have sat on both Planning & Residents Services Committees.

•Community Advocacy: Dedicated to representing diverse ward interests and securing improvements in local infrastructure and services.

•Business Management: Over 30 years of experience as a Director in the private sector, overseeing operations and strategic growth.

Following the failure of Conservatives & Independents in Yeading is am standing in the ward to give residents assistance and badly needed representation.

I bring passion and experience as a Development Advocate with a track record of impact and commitment to rights-based and people-centred approaches.

I have been a resident in Hillingdon Council for 20 years and have made Yiewsley my home for 10 years. In the wider Hillingdon, I served 10 years on the Independent Education Appeals Panel helping to ensure families are accorded fair processes and statutory compliance by the Local Authority in Education Service provision. I have also supported public and policy campaigns on Climate Change, No Third Runway and Stop HS2, for their environmental effects on Hillingdon communities.

I know Yiewsley and I see Yiewsley's potential as a thriving diverse community. I understand the local issues and have supported efforts on community integration for years. In the last year, since expressing my interest to serve as your Councillor, I have been engaging residents and businesses, listening to issues and taking notes of proposed solutions. I have reported local issues to relevant authorities, called incumbent Councillors to action and effectively influenced action.

A vote for me, MOSES Elizabeth Oluwakemi, is a vote to strengthen the work I have already begun - service to Yiewsley - and a vote to keep the ears, heart and hands of Conservative-led Hillingdon Council closer and closer to Yiewsley. TANJORE Sriram has actively worked together with me and a vote for him also will ensure Yiewsley has 2 Councillors with aligned goals and consequently, better results. Give us the official mandate to do the job.

If successfully elected as your Councillor for Yiewsley, I want to work with local stakeholders for a revitalised and sustainable Yiewsley, including:

Cleaner Yiewsley: Working with residents to co-own responsibility for making every street in Yiewsley clean, to ensure clean green spaces, and to report fly-tipping offences.

Healthier Yiewsley: Ensuring support for social, health and environmental services to residents and improv
